I want to focus on straight line syntax for a really long time that I said this 11. Let me explain exactly what I mean. Those of you really are familiar with this. The straight line syntax represents the order in which you go about creating certainty. In other words, sales at the highest level, right, is the transference of emotion. And the primary emotion that you're transferring is certainty, right? The certainty about your product, certainty about yourself that, you know, certainly your product is awesome. It's 10, certainly make yourself a second 10, certainly about the company standing behind the product of third 10, right? You're transferring that certainty to another human being. That's what happens when you sell. Now how you actually go about doing that, we have steps. So imagine like your goal, essentially, you know, broadly speaking is that as you move someone down the straight line, they open to the clothes. What you're doing basically is everything you say, everything you do, every document, every sales aid you use, whatever it might be, you're looking to build more and more certainty for each of the three 10s. That's it. That's what's going on. And then eventually lowering the action threshold and then adding on pain. Now what the straight line does is it gives you a step by step formula for doing that the same way every time. And the reason you're one day, if you're new to the system or if you're being compromised for reinforcement, when you look on the site, the networking site and you see all these wacky slur like people saying I changed their lives or the straight line did this, the straight line does that. The reason why it's so effective is because it's literally the old, I've never seen one, another system is even close to it. It's an end to end system. It addresses every aspect of the sale from the first forward, addie, mouth to the moment either they buy or you end the conversation, right? Either with a callback, you ever speak them again. It's all covered with the straight line and also covered with three forms of communication, tonality, by language and the words you say. It wraps up all forms of communication and handles the entire sale from open to close. Now remember the key here in terms of like going off the Pluto or your anus, right? Is that when you get caught up in sort of taking your prospects answer, whether you ask a question during your intelligence guys, got it, and then they answer that question and it's like drifting off the Pluto and you jump in because they like me because the water's warm and you start going off the Pluto yourself, right? The key is that rather than doing that, you want to list the people as they spiral if you want to listen to them and you're like, oh, wow, you're actually listening and then you want to bring them back to the straight lines and say, oh, wow, that's great. Then you say, and then you go back to the next question and you're an intelligent guy, right? Once your prospects spirals off or interrupts you, you answer the question and you're, oh, wow, and you move back to the point in the conversation where you left off. You're staying straight along the syntax. So let's go through what syntax actually is. Step one, you must take immediate control of the sale, right? Why? Because taking control opens up the possibility that you can make every sale the same. Once you're in control, you can go about picking the lock on his buying strategy in the most efficient, most elegant manner. That takes you to step two and three together. It's a double headed step of intelligence gathering, right? And report building. Remember, the way we build massive rapport is through gathering intelligence. Now, obviously, even though first for seconds, we'll look into start building rapport, but that second step of gathering intelligence, it's crucial that we do it in a way that positions us as a trusted family doctor, the trusted family friend, the advisor versus the grand inquisitor. The grand inquisitor is out of rapport and not trusted. The family friend is trusted. The advisor is trusted and it will open up to you. You may import open to the possibility of close, right? Now, those two steps it ends with after you've exhausted all the questions you need to ask. You understand what their needs are, their values, their beliefs, their experience of other salespeople, experience of other products similar to yours, and also where their pain lies, which is huge. What's their deepest worry? What's that part of the skills out of control because of a lack of this product? And understand that pain now essentially is going to be something we hold in our artists. We don't resolve the pain just yet. We hold it in our artists and we'll use that at the end of the sale to lower the action special and do our last loop. Okay?
